stories.title: **Headline:** Ohtani On-Base Streak Passes 48 Games — Chasing Dodgers Franchise History

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani's on-base streak: 48 games. 🔥

Already broke Ichiro's Japanese-born MLB record (44).
Already passed Ron Cey (47) for 3rd in Dodgers franchise history.

Next milestone: Willie Keeler at 50.

He also has a 0.00 ERA through two starts this year. #Dodgers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
For context on Ohtani's 48-game streak: 🔥

All-time MLB record: Ted Williams, 84 games (1949).
Dodgers franchise leaders ahead: Keeler (50), Green (53), Snider (58).

And he's also posting a 0.00 ERA as a pitcher.

Baseball doesn't have a better story. #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Ohtani's On-Base Streak Reaches 48 Games — Chasing Dodgers Franchise History

stories.title: **Headline:** Walker vs. Judge — Baseball's Best April HR Race, Both at 8

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
MLB home run leaderboard through April 16: 🔥

1. Jordan Walker (Cardinals) — 8 HRs, 11-game hit streak
1. Aaron Judge (Yankees) — 8 HRs

Judge is on a 60-HR pace.
Walker had 11 total HRs in his first TWO MLB seasons combined.

April is wild. #Cardinals #Yankees #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Jordan Walker ties a Cardinals franchise record. 🔥

8 home runs in the first 16 games.
Only McGwire, Musial, Rolen, and Pujols had done it before him.

Then Aaron Judge hit his 8th on Thursday and pulled even.

Walker: wRC+ 219, 97.1 mph exit velo — MLB-best. #Cardinals #MLB

article.headline: Walker vs. Judge — Baseball's April Home Run Race You Weren't Expecting

stories.title: **Headline:** Giolito Signs with Braves — One-Year, $6M Deal Ends Mid-Spring Free Agency

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Lucas Giolito has signed with the Atlanta Braves. 1 year, $6 million.

The Cubs and Padres were the last teams linked to him.
The Braves (12-7, NL East leaders) get a proven arm.

Giolito went 10-4, 3.41 ERA in 26 starts for the Red Sox in 2025. #Braves #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Brewers Skid Continues — How NL Central Race Changed in Two Weeks

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Brewers have lost 6 straight — their longest skid since 2003. 🔥

On the IL: Yelich (May), Chourio (early May), Vaughn (mid-May), Priester, Koenig.

Pittsburgh and Cincinnati: both 11-7, tied for NL Central lead.

The division just flipped. #Brewers #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Trevor Story's Return — 5 RBIs, Homer, Red Sox Rout Twins

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Trevor Story: home run + 5 RBIs as the Red Sox beat the Twins on Tuesday. 🔥

After UCL surgery, limited seasons, and question marks — Story looks healthy and dangerous in 2026.

This is what a real comeback looks like. #RedSox #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** The 2026 Standings After 18 Games — Who's Running Away

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Three weeks into the 2026 MLB season: 🔥

Dodgers: 14-4 (best in baseball)
Braves: 12-7 (NL East)
Twins: 11-8 (AL Central)
Pirates/Reds: 11-7 tied (NL Central)
Tampa Bay: 10-7 (AL East)

Biggest surprise: Pittsburgh and Cincinnati running the NL Central. #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** ABS Challenge System — Three Weeks In, Players Still Figuring It Out

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Three weeks in, players are still adjusting to MLB's automated ball-strike system. 🔥

ESPN: "It's harder than it looks on TV."

Some hitters love the accountability. Some pitchers miss the gray area.

Baseball's biggest rules change in years. #MLB
